Estimate the time taken for a linear search vs. binary search. **Solution — Linear Search:** $$ T_{linear} = N \times t_c = 10^7 \times 10^{-6} = 10\,\text{seconds} $$ **Solution — Binary Search (sorted database):** $$ T_{binary} = \log_2(N) \times t_c = \log_2(10^7) \times 10^{-6} \approx 23.25 \times 10^{-6}\,\text{s} \approx 23.25\,\mu\text{s} $$ > **Conclusion:** This is why real-world antivirus engines use **hash-based indexing (B-trees, Bloom filters)** instead of linear scanning. ## 3.2 KTU Analytical Decision Matrix > [!IMPORTANT] > **This matrix is a high-yield KTU 2024 revision tool.** Memorize the action-recommended mapping. | Detected Condition | Recommended Action | Justification | |--------------------|-------------------|---------------| | Signature match with **known virus** | **Quarantine → Delete** | Prevent re-infection | | Heuristic score $S_{score} \geq 70$ | **Block + Quarantine** | High probability of zero-day malware | | Heuristic score $30 \leq S_{score} < 70$ | **Quarantine + User Alert** | Suspicious; needs manual review | | File in trusted system directory | **Skip scan** (whitelisted) | Avoid false positives on OS files | | Scan interrupted by shutdown | **Resume on next boot** | Ensure full disk coverage | | Update server unreachable | **Use last cached definitions** | Maintain protection with warning | | Two antivirus programs detected | **Disable one** | Prevent resource conflict | ## 3.3 Comparative Analysis: Scan Types | Scan Type | Scope | Duration | Detection Rate | Use Case | |-----------|-------|----------|----------------|----------| | **Quick Scan** | Boot sector, RAM, critical folders | 2–15 min | ~70% | Daily/weekly routine | | **Full Scan** | All files, all drives | 1–5 hrs | ~99% | Monthly deep clean | | **Custom Scan** | User-selected paths | Variable | Variable | Targeted investigation | | **Boot-Time Scan** | Pre-OS load, rootkits | 30–60 min | ~95% | Persistent malware | | **On-Demand Scan** | Manual trigger | Variable | High | Suspicious file inspection | | **Real-Time Scan** | All I/O operations | Continuous | Very High | Always-on protection | # SECTION 4: Structural Diagrams & Schematics ## 4.1 Functional Block Diagram: Antivirus Engine Architecture > [!NOTE] > The following Mermaid block diagram illustrates the **end-to-end functional flow** of a virus protection program from file interception to remediation. ```mermaid flowchart TD A[File Entering System
Download, USB, Email] --> B[Kernel Mode Minifilter Driver
Intercepts I/O Request] B --> C{Real Time Protection
Enabled?} C -- No --> Z1[File Allowed Unchecked] C -- Yes --> D[Compute Cryptographic Hash
SHA-256 Algorithm] D --> E[Query Signature Database
Local VDF File] E --> F{Signature Match
Found?} F -- Yes --> G[Mark File as INFECTED] F -- No --> H[Launch Heuristic Engine
Sandbox Execution] H --> I[Monitor API Calls
and Behavior Patterns] I --> J[Compute Suspicion Score] J --> K{Score Above
Threshold T_high?} K -- Yes --> G K -- No --> L{Score Between
T_low and T_high?} L -- Yes --> M[Flag as SUSPICIOUS] L -- No --> N[Mark File as CLEAN] G --> O[Apply User Selected Action] M --> O N --> P[File Allowed to Execute] O --> Q{User Action} Q -- Quarantine --> R[Encrypt and Move to
Quarantine Vault] Q -- Delete --> S[Secure File Erasure] Q -- Clean --> T[Attempt Malware Removal] R --> U[Generate Scan Log Report] S --> U T --> U P --> V[Background Update Engine
Sync with Cloud Server] V --> E ``` ## 4.2 Sequence Diagram: User-Initiated Full System Scan ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant U as User participant UI as Antivirus GUI participant SE as Scan Engine participant DB as Signature Database participant HE as Heuristic Module participant FS as File System participant QV as Quarantine Vault U->>UI: Click "Full Scan" button UI->>SE: Initiate scan request SE->>DB: Check for latest updates DB-->>SE: Definitions current / Update available SE->>FS: Enumerate all files and directories loop For each file F SE->>FS: Read file content SE->>SE: Compute SHA-256 hash SE->>DB: Lookup hash alt Match found DB-->>SE: INFECTED signature match SE->>QV: Quarantine file else No match SE->>HE: Run heuristic analysis HE-->>SE: Suspicion score alt Score high SE->>QV: Quarantine file else Score low SE-->>UI: File is CLEAN end end end SE->>UI: Scan complete - Display report UI-->>U: Show threats found, actions taken ``` ## 4.3 Decision Tree: Choosing the Right Scan Type ```mermaid flowchart TD Start([User Wants to Run Antivirus]) --> Q1{Is system
showing symptoms?} Q1 -- Yes slow crashes popups --> S1[Full System Scan] Q1 -- No routine check --> Q2{Time available?} Q2 -- Less than 15 min --> S2[Quick Scan] Q2 -- More than 1 hour --> S3[Full System Scan] Q1 -- Suspected single file --> S4[Custom Scan on specific path] S1 --> Update[Update Definitions First] S2 --> Update S3 --> Update S4 --> Update Update --> Run[Click Scan Now] Run --> Monitor[Monitor Progress] Monitor --> Review[Review Results] Review --> Action{Take Action} Action --> Clean[Clean / Quarantine / Delete] Action --> Schedule[Schedule Next Scan] ``` ## 4.4 Cloud Update Flow Diagram ```mermaid flowchart LR A[Antivirus Client] -->|HTTPS GET| B[Vendor Cloud Server] B -->|JSON Signature Pack| A A --> C[Local VDF Database] C --> D[Signature Matcher] D --> E[Updated Protection Status] ``` # SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap ## 5.1 Part A: Short Answer Questions (3 Marks Each) ### Question 1 **[KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]** **Q: Define a Virus Protection Program. List any four types of malware it can detect.** **Model Answer (3 Marks):** > **Definition (1.5 Marks):** A Virus Protection Program, commonly known as **Antivirus Software**, is a utility application designed to detect, prevent, identify, and remove malicious software (malware) from a computer system. It uses techniques such as **signature-based detection, heuristic analysis, and behavioral monitoring** to safeguard the system. > **Four Types of Malware (1.5 Marks — 0.375 each):** > 1. **Virus** — Self-replicating code that attaches to legitimate programs. > 2. **Worm** — Standalone malware that spreads across networks autonomously. > 3. **Trojan Horse** — Malware disguised as legitimate software. > 4. **Ransomware** — Malware that encrypts user data and demands ransom. --- ### Question 2 **[KTU University Exam - June 2024]** **Q: What is the difference between a Quick Scan and a Full System Scan?** **Model Answer (3 Marks):** | Parameter | Quick Scan | Full System Scan | |-----------|-----------|------------------| | **Scope** | Boot sector, RAM, registry, critical system folders | All files on all drives including archives | | **Duration** | 2–15 minutes | 1–5 hours | | **Detection Rate** | ~70% (covers active threats) | ~99% (comprehensive) | | **Use Case** | Daily/weekly routine check | Monthly deep clean or post-infection | | **Marks Distribution:** 1.5 marks for tabular differences + 1.5 marks for the right column with proper keywords. | --- ## 5.2 Part B: Descriptive Questions (14 Marks — Module Internal Choice) ### Question A (14 Marks) **[KTU University Exam - Model Question, 2024 Scheme]** **Q: (a)** Explain the step-by-step procedure to run a virus protection program on a Windows-based computer. Include the various scan types available. **(7 Marks)** **(b)** What is heuristic analysis in antivirus software? Explain how it detects zero-day malware. Why is updating the virus definition database important? **(7 Marks)** #### Model Solution — Part (a) [7 Marks] **Step 1 — Verify Installation (1 Mark):** Open the Control Panel → Programs → Confirm a valid antivirus (e.g., Windows Defender, Avast) is installed and active. **Step 2 — Update Virus Definitions (1 Mark):** Click "Check for updates" in the antivirus interface. This downloads the latest malware signatures from the vendor's cloud server via HTTPS on port 443. Without updates, the antivirus cannot detect newly discovered threats. **Step 3 — Choose Scan Type (2 Marks — 0.5 each + 0.5 for connection):** - **Quick Scan:** Scans critical areas like boot sectors, registry, and system folders. Recommended for routine checks. - **Full System Scan:** Scans every file on all connected drives. Time-consuming but thorough. - **Custom Scan:** User selects specific files, folders, or drives to scan. - **Boot-Time Scan:** Runs before the OS loads to detect rootkits and persistent malware. **Step 4 — Initiate Scan (1 Mark):** Click the "Scan Now" button. The antivirus engine reads each file, computes a SHA-256 hash, and compares it with the signature database. **Step 5 — Review Results and Take Action (1 Mark):** After the scan completes, review the list of detected threats. For each infected file, choose from: **Clean** (remove malware code), **Quarantine** (isolate), **Delete** (permanent removal), or **Allow** (false positive). **Step 6 — Generate Report and Schedule (1 Mark):** Save the scan log (in `.txt`/`.xml` format) for records. Configure scheduled scans via Windows Task Scheduler to automate future protection. #### Model Solution — Part (b) [7 Marks] **Heuristic Analysis — Definition (2 Marks):** > Heuristic Analysis is an **advanced detection technique** used by antivirus software to identify **previously unknown malware (zero-day threats)** by analyzing the **behavioral patterns, code structure, and API call sequences** of a program, rather than relying solely on known signatures. **Working Principle (3 Marks — 1.5 each for mechanism + score):** 1. The suspected file is executed inside a **sandboxed virtual environment** (an isolated emulator). 2. The heuristic engine monitors all system calls, file operations, registry modifications, and network activity. 3. A **suspicion score** $S_{score}$ is calculated: $$ S_{score} = \sum_{i=1}^{k} w_i \cdot A_i $$ If $S_{score}$ exceeds a **threshold $T_{high}$** (typically 70), the file is flagged as malicious and **quarantined**, even though no signature exists in the database. **Importance of Updating Virus Definitions (2 Marks):** - New malware is created **every few seconds** worldwide. - Signature-based detection **cannot catch** a threat whose hash is not in the database. - Updates provide the latest **malware fingerprints, behavioral rules, and heuristic weights**. - Without updates, the antivirus becomes **progressively ineffective** — like a security guard with an outdated "most wanted" list. --- ### Question B (14 Marks) — Alternative Choice **[KTU University Exam - Model Question, 2024 Scheme]** **Q: (a)** What is a computer virus? Explain the different types of viruses with examples. **(7 Marks)** **(b)** Describe the architecture of a modern antivirus program with a neat block diagram. Explain the function of the quarantine module. **(7 Marks)** #### Model Solution — Part (a) [7 Marks] **Definition of Computer Virus (2 Marks):** > A **computer virus** is a type of malicious software that **self-replicates** by inserting its code into other legitimate programs or files. It requires **user action** (e.g., opening an infected file) to spread and can cause data corruption, system slowdown, or unauthorized access. **Types of Viruses (5 Marks — 1 each):** 1. **Boot Sector Virus:** Infects the **Master Boot Record (MBR)** of a hard disk. Loads before the operating system. Example: **Brain Virus**. 2. **File Infector Virus:** Attaches itself to **executable files** (`.exe`, `.com`). Activates when the program is run. Example: **CIH Virus**. 3. **Macro Virus:** Targets **macro-enabled documents** (e.g., MS Word, Excel). Example: **Melissa Virus**. 4. **Polymorphic Virus:** **Encrypts itself** with a different key each time, changing its signature to evade detection. Example: **Storm Worm**. 5. **Multipartite Virus:** Combines **boot sector and file infector** techniques, spreading through multiple vectors. Example: **Natas Virus**. #### Model Solution — Part (b) [7 Marks] **Architecture of Modern Antivirus (4 Marks):** A modern antivirus consists of **five core modules**: 1. **Real-Time Monitor:** Continuously intercepts all file and network I/O operations. 2. **Signature Engine:** Compares file hashes against a database of known malware signatures. 3. **Heuristic Engine:** Analyzes code behavior in a sandbox to detect unknown threats. 4. **Quarantine Module:** Isolates infected files in a secure, encrypted vault. 5. **Update Engine:** Downloads the latest virus definitions from the cloud server. **Block Diagram (2 Marks):** [Refer to SECTION 4.1 Mermaid diagram above for the full block diagram] **Function of the Quarantine Module (1 Mark):** The Quarantine Module **encrypts infected files using AES-256** and relocates them to a protected directory (e.g., `C:\Quarantine`). This serves three purposes: - **Prevents the malware from executing** and causing further harm. - **Preserves the file** for potential recovery if it is a false positive. - **Isolates the threat** from the rest of the system, limiting its ability to spread. --- > [!WARNING] > **KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning / Common Pitfall Callout** > > **1. Do NOT confuse "Virus" with "Antivirus".** A virus is the *threat*; antivirus is the *defense*. Examiners deduct 1 full mark for this fundamental confusion. > > **2. Avoid writing "I run the antivirus" without specifying the SCAN TYPE.** Always mention whether it is a Quick Scan, Full Scan, Custom Scan, or Boot-Time Scan. Examiners explicitly look for this distinction. > > **3. Skipping the "Update Definitions" step is a guaranteed 1-mark deduction.** It is the most commonly tested procedural step. > > **4. In Part B questions, failing to draw a block diagram costs 2 marks.** Even a simple labeled block diagram earns partial credit. Use the Mermaid-style or hand-drawn block diagram format accepted by KTU. > > **5. Do not write generic definitions** like "Antivirus removes viruses." Use precise KTU terminology: *"detects, prevents, identifies, quarantines, and removes malicious software using signature-based and heuristic techniques."* --- ## 5.3 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember > [!IMPORTANT] > **High-Density Rapid Revision Checklist for KTU 2024 ESE** - ✅ A **Virus Protection Program (Antivirus)** is utility software that **detects, prevents, quarantines, and removes malware**. - ✅ The **standard procedure** is: *Verify → Update Definitions → Choose Scan Type → Run Scan → Review → Take Action → Schedule Next Scan*. - ✅ **Three main scan types:** Quick Scan, Full System Scan, Custom Scan. - ✅ **Signature-based detection** uses cryptographic hashes (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256) compared against a **virus definition database**. - ✅ **Heuristic analysis** detects **zero-day malware** by monitoring behavioral patterns in a sandboxed environment using a **suspicion score threshold**. - ✅ **Quarantine** isolates infected files by **AES-256 encryption** in a protected vault. - ✅ **Real-time protection** must be **enabled at all times** for maximum security. - ✅ **Definition updates** should be performed **every 4–24 hours** via **HTTPS on port 443**. - ✅ **Never run two antivirus programs simultaneously** — causes software conflicts and system instability. - ✅ **Common malware types:** Virus, Worm, Trojan, Ransomware, Spyware, Rootkit, Adware. - ✅ **Windows Defender CLI tool:** `MpCmdRun.exe` located at `C:\ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows Defender\Platform\\`. - ✅ **Scan logs** are saved in `.txt`, `.xml`, or `.json` format for audit and forensic purposes. - ✅ **Recommended scan frequency:** Quick Scan weekly + Full Scan monthly. - ✅ **KTU Bloom's Level:** Apply (L3) and Analyze (L4) — focus on **"how to run"** rather than just "what is it." - ✅ **CO Mapping:** Directly linked to **CO4** of OEEVT612 — *Apply preventive maintenance procedures.*
